status_legend.in_progress: The feature is the current active task.
status_legend.blocked: Work cannot continue until a documented blocker is resolved.
status_legend.passing: Required verification has passed and evidence is recorded.

task_status_legend.in_progress: Subtask is being worked on right now.
task_status_legend.blocked: Subtask cannot continue until a documented blocker clears.
task_status_legend.passing: Subtask is implemented and its verification passed.

link_semantics.depends_on: This feature/task cannot start until the listed ids reach status 'passing'.
link_semantics.blocks: The listed ids cannot start until this feature/task reaches status 'passing'. Should be the reciprocal of depends_on.
link_semantics.tasks: Kanban sub-tickets. The planner writes them from its blueprint; the conductor flips their status as the generator/executor complete them. Each task is itself a small unit with its own status, owner agent, files, and dependencies.
link_semantics.acceptance_criteria: Checkable definition of done. Each item flips done=true only when its evidence is recorded.

features.title: Add Phase 0: Grill — requirements discovery before planning
features.user_visible_behavior: Before any feature is planned or implemented, the harness interviews the user relentlessly about the new goal across five dimensions (Problem Space, Happy Path, Error Cases, Constraints, Acceptance Criteria). Questions are asked one at a time. Structured choices ('chiochs') are presented for key decisions. A spec is written to specs/<feature-id>.md and requires human approval before the feature enters the pipeline. No code or planning happens until the spec is approved.

features.title: Make the workflow mechanical: gates, live ledger, kanban tasks, agent evidence
features.user_visible_behavior: Invoking harness-engineering on a build request runs a hard Gate Sequence: bootstrap check, then a Spec Gate that invokes grill when no approved spec exists, then a live phase ledger the conductor ticks off so no step is dropped. The planner's task breakdown is persisted into feature_list.json tasks[] (kanban sub-tickets) rather than evaporating in chat. Features link to each other via depends_on/blocks. Each of the 5 agents must invoke its required skill (e.g. code-architect runs code-quality-audit for non-trivial features) and emit a proof-of-invocation evidence line. Two new hooks force grill on build requests and block agent spawns before spec+ledger exist.

acceptance_criteria.text: Build request + no approved spec triggers grill before any planning (Spec Gate in conductor + spec-gate hook)

features.notes: Verified (init.sh 58/58); WIP slot released to code-quality-language. Commit pending user. Addresses four reported failures: (1) steps dropped mid-run, (2) feature_list too flat / no ticket links, (3) grill not triggered on invocation, (4) agents skipping their required skills. Design forks chosen by the user: schema=tasks+links, enforcement=gates+ledger+hooks; then expanded to 8 agents (explorer/reviewer/scribe).

features.title: Make code-quality language-agnostic — one universal language skill, infer per language
features.user_visible_behavior: code-quality keeps the same 16 design principles + 13 patterns and the same 11 tooling rules, but applies them to ANY language by inferring that language's idioms from the repo (type system, enums, DI, logging, error model, linter/formatter). The two per-language skills (code-quality:python, code-quality:rust) are replaced by a single code-quality-language skill; Python and Rust remain as the two worked examples (references/python, references/rust). The router, audit, fix, and review sub-skills are de-Pythonized to neutral intents. The generator agent and conductor invoke code-quality-language.

features.title: Catch real-use bugs: adversarial correctness review, mandatory E2E + regression testing, trigger-based security review
features.user_visible_behavior: The pipeline's checkers verified structure and the-tests-that-exist, never real behavior, so code passed tests but was buggy in use, fixes caused regressions, no E2E tests existed, and there was no security review. GATE 11 becomes a review cluster: reviewer (quality) + correctness-reviewer (adversarial behavior — traces every control/data path, enumerates edge cases, hunts logic bugs, and proves each acceptance criterion with a real test) always for non-trivial work, plus security-reviewer when a security trigger fires (auth, untrusted input, query/command building, network/file I/O, deserialization, crypto/secrets, new dependency). The verify pipeline makes Layer 3 (E2E) mandatory for user-facing work; the generator writes the E2E workflow test first then unit tests; the executor runs the full suite (no fail-fast) and rejects missing-E2E / AC-uncovered handoffs; the healer adds a failing-first regression test on every fix and re-runs the full suite. Wired into the conductor, the pre-agent-spawn hook, and the deterministic tiger-pipeline.js workflow.

acceptance_criteria.text: correctness-reviewer agent (opus) exists with the correctness-review proof line and control-flow trace + edge-case + logic-bug + AC-to-test sections
code-correctness-review skill exists (name=code-correctness-review) defining the adversarial protocol, checklist, and proof line
harness-engineering-verify makes Layer 3 (E2E) mandatory for user-facing features; completion gate requires E2E evidence + an asserting test per AC
generator writes E2E-first + unit and cannot hand off a user-facing feature without an E2E test; executor runs the full suite (no fail-fast) and rejects missing-E2E / AC-uncovered handoffs
healer adds a failing-first regression test on every fix (E2E for user-visible bugs) and re-runs the full unit + E2E suite
security-reviewer agent (opus) + security-review skill exist, trigger-based, with a trigger table, 12-category checklist, severity model, and proof line
conductor GATE 11 runs the review cluster; proof-of-invocation table + agents-at-a-glance list 10 agents; pre-agent-spawn hook lists all 10
plugin.json lists 15 skills at v4.7.0; marketplace/AGENTS/CLAUDE/README + tiger-pipeline.js updated; ./init.sh passes with 10 agents + 15 skills

features.notes: Addresses the reported failure: agents ship code that passes its tests but is buggy in real use, fixes cause regressions, no E2E tests, review passes too easily, and no security review existed. User design forks (structured choices): correctness=dedicated adversarial agent; tests=unit+E2E, focus on E2E; regression=failing-first test + full-suite rerun; security=separate agent + skill, trigger-based; process=spec first. Baked into the deterministic workflow per user request.
